title: Database schema migration for personal event ownership
body:
## Issue description

This child issue of the **Personal Event Ownership Epic** tracks the database schema changes required to shift from group-owned to user-owned events. The migration must:

- Add explicit `owner_id` column to the `events` table
- Make `group_id` nullable so events can exist without a group association
- Create the `event_shares` junction table for multi-group sharing with visibility levels
- Backfill `owner_id` from `created_by` for all existing events
- Backfill `event_shares` for existing group-scoped events to maintain backward compatibility
- Add appropriate indexes for query performance

### Current state

Migration `20260328000010_personal_event_ownership.ts` has been implemented with:
- `owner_id` (UUID, NOT NULL, FK → user_profiles) added to `events`
- `group_id` made nullable on `events`
- `event_shares` table created with `event_id`, `group_id`, `visibility_level`, `shared_by_user_id`, timestamps, and a unique constraint on `[event_id, group_id]`
- Backfill of `owner_id` from `created_by`
- Backfill of `event_shares` from existing group-scoped events with `visibility_level = 'details'`
- Indexes: `idx_events_owner_id`, `idx_event_shares_group_id`, `idx_event_shares_shared_by_user_id`

### Remaining work

- Verify migration runs cleanly from scratch and rolls back correctly
- Verify existing migration integration tests pass with the new schema
- Add dedicated integration tests for the personal event ownership migration

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. The `events.owner_id` column must be NOT NULL and reference `user_profiles(id)`
2. The `events.group_id` column must be nullable to support personal events
3. The `event_shares` table must enforce a unique constraint on `(event_id, group_id)`
4. The `event_shares.visibility_level` column must only accept `busy`, `title`, or `details`
5. Backfill must set `owner_id = created_by` for all existing events
6. Backfill must create `event_shares` rows for all existing group-scoped events
7. Migration must be reversible (down migration drops columns/tables and restores constraints)
8. All production indexes must be present for performance
9. The migration must not break existing queries or data

---

## Additional notes

### Key files
- Migration: `db/migrations/20260328000010_personal_event_ownership.ts`
- Migration tests: `src/infrastructure/personal-event-ownership.migration.integration.test.ts`
- General migration tests: `src/infrastructure/migrations.integration.test.ts`
- Event repository: `src/modules/event-management/infrastructure/knex-event.repository.ts`
- Event share repository: `src/modules/event-sharing/infrastructure/knex-event-share.repository.ts`

### Constraints
- Follow the existing migration pattern established in earlier migrations
- Use Knex migration API consistently
- The `owner_id` backfill must handle all edge cases (no orphan events)
- The `event_shares` backfill must not create duplicate shares

### Parent epic
Personal Event Ownership — Shift from Group-Owned to User-Owned Events

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration `20260328000010_personal_event_ownership.ts` creates `owner_id` column on `events` (NOT NULL, FK to user_profiles)
- [ ] Migration makes `events.group_id` nullable
- [ ] Migration creates `event_shares` table with correct schema and constraints
- [ ] Backfill correctly sets `owner_id = created_by` for all existing events
- [ ] Backfill creates `event_shares` for existing group-scoped events with `visibility_level = 'details'`
- [ ] Down migration cleanly reverses all changes
- [ ] Production indexes exist: `idx_events_owner_id`, `idx_event_shares_group_id`, `idx_event_shares_shared_by_user_id`
- [ ] Integration tests verify migration runs from scratch
- [ ] Integration tests verify rollback works correctly
- [ ] All existing migration tests continue to pass
- [ ] CI passes
